summary: Matched HSD_AObjAlloc in C by replacing the assert string literal "0" with the existing small-data label lbl_804D5D10, allowing removal of the NON_MATCHING/inline assembly fallback. The PR also removed now-unnecessary peephole pragma push/pop blocks around HSD_AObjFree and archive public-address/external-location routines.
what_changed: In src/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.c, removed a #pragma pop before HSD_AObjAlloc and a #pragma push/#pragma peephole on before HSD_AObjFree.
Converted HSD_AObjAlloc from a NON_MATCHING C implementation plus inline assembly fallback into a single matching C implementation.
Changed the allocation-failure assertion in HSD_AObjAlloc from __assert(lbl_804D5D08, 489, "0") to __assert(lbl_804D5D08, 489, lbl_804D5D10).
Removed the inline PowerPC assembly version of HSD_AObjAlloc, including the decomp.me scratch reference comment.
In src/sysdolphin/baselib/archive.c, removed #pragma push/#pragma peephole on before HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress and the matching #pragma pop after HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern.
smart_moves: The key matching fix was recognizing that the assert argument needed to reference lbl_804D5D10 rather than emitting or selecting the literal string "0" directly.
Once HSD_AObjAlloc matched, the PR deleted the asm fallback entirely instead of leaving stale NON_MATCHING scaffolding.
The cleanup removed peephole pragmas only where they were no longer needed, reducing compiler-control noise without changing function logic.

The matched C version preserves the original allocation flow: HSD_ObjAlloc, null assert, memset over sizeof(HSD_AObj), AOBJ_NO_ANIM flag initialization, framerate set to 1.0f, and return of the allocated object.
decomp_lessons: For assert calls, matching may depend on using the same string object or label as the original binary, not merely an equivalent C string literal.
Small-data labels such as lbl_804D5D08 and lbl_804D5D10 can be necessary evidence for reproducing exact codegen in baselib routines.
After a function is matched, remove NON_MATCHING guards and inline asm to keep the source tree searchable and maintainable.
Pragma push/pop and peephole toggles should be revisited after matching work; they may have been local workarounds rather than permanent requirements.
Simple allocator routines can often be matched by checking not only control flow and assignments but also exact constants, assert parameters, and data placement.
naming_conventions: Baselib allocator/free routines use the HSD_AObjAlloc and HSD_AObjFree naming pattern for AObj lifecycle management.
Object allocator backing data is named aobj_alloc_data and passed to HSD_ObjAlloc.
AObj default flags use the named constant AOBJ_NO_ANIM rather than a raw 0x40000000 literal in C.
Unidentified or compiler-generated string/data labels remain in lbl_XXXXXXXX form when no semantic name is established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace inline assembly with equivalent C once exact codegen is achieved.
Use existing labels for assert message/file arguments when string literal spelling alone changes placement or addressing.
Compare the generated null-check branch and __assert call arguments against the assembly before deleting NON_MATCHING.
Preserve initialization order to match assembly: allocate, assert on null, memset, set flags, set framerate, return.
Remove peephole pragmas when matching no longer depends on them.
follow_up_queries: Find other __assert calls using string literal "0" that may need an existing lbl_ small-data symbol for matching.
Search for remaining #pragma peephole on blocks in src/sysdolphin/baselib that may be removable after matching.
Search for remaining NON_MATCHING allocator routines that follow the HSD_ObjAlloc, memset, default-fields pattern.
Compare other AObj or Obj lifecycle functions for similar unnecessary pragma push/pop scaffolding.
